stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
Key associated technologies include transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of 'meta-llama' indicates relevance to major industry players in large language models.

stories.title: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Amazon's cybersecurity research reportedly influenced a directive leading to Anthropic restricting access to its Fable and Mythos models.
The decision involved discussions between Amazon CEO Andy Jassy and the White House regarding export controls.
This highlights the intersection of corporate security research, government regulation, and advanced AI model access.

stories.title: Anthropic cuts off Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access following government order
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Anthropic has completely cut off access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models.
The action was taken in response to a government order citing national security concerns.
The restriction applies globally, blocking access for all foreign nations, including Anthropic employees.

stories.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has ceased operation of its Fable and Mythos models.
The shutdown was reportedly due to a directive from the Trump administration's Commerce Department.
Concerns center on the potential for a Fable 5 'jailbreak' to pose a national security risk.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability exists in PeopleSoft software, which is owned by Oracle.
The exploit allows unauthorized access leading to the theft of gigabytes of data.
The vulnerability affects hundreds of organizations, indicating widespread potential impact.

stories.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Ukraine utilized fully autonomous drones in a recent test to engage and kill Russian soldiers.
The report highlights Ukraine's integration of AI modules into its drone and robotic systems.
The use of full autonomy in this context represents a significant, real-world application of advanced AI in warfare.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces EurekAgent, an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
The research suggests the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to environment engineering (resources, constraints, interfaces).
LLM agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions that can outperform human-designed methods.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces EurekAgent, an approach focusing on Agent Environment Engineering for autonomous scientific discovery.
LLM-based agents are capable of proposing, validating, and iterating scientific solutions that can surpass human-designed methods.
The research suggests the primary bottleneck for future autonomous discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to environment design (resources, constraints, interfaces).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 architecture, indicating ongoing development in large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration enables faster performance when running Ollama specifically on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
This architecture emphasizes privacy and local execution, contrasting with cloud-based AI services.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, running locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Microsoft hasn’t ruled out spinning off Xbox
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly planning significant layoffs within its Xbox division.
The company is reevaluating the roadmap and plans for the next-generation Project Helix console.
Microsoft is considering a major restructuring of Xbox, including the possibility of spinning it off into a separate entity.

stories.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Agents-K1 introduces a new end-to-end pipeline designed for scientific knowledge orchestration, moving beyond simple LLM agent orchestration.
The system addresses a critical gap in current LLM agents by capturing detailed scientific elements (entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms) rather than just abstracts or citation graphs.
This advancement aims to enable deeper, more rigorous scientific reasoning capabilities within AI agents.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) demonstrate that recursion over model calls is effective for long-context reasoning.
A new pattern, the 'Recursive Agent Harness,' is identified, where the recursive unit is a full agent (with tools/filesystem) rather than just a model call.
This concept is being adopted in production coding agents, exemplified by Anthropic's dynamic workflows.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across diverse hardware configurations.
It strengthens Ollama's MLX engine specifically for Apple silicon devices.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: My first 24 hours with Siri AI on the Mac
stories.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
bullet_points: The article reviews the initial experience with the new Siri AI integration on macOS 27 Golden Gate.
The author expresses prior skepticism toward Siri and Apple Intelligence, suggesting the new version warrants reconsideration.
The piece is an early-stage hands-on test, indicating the feature is new and under active development/testing.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX has transitioned to a publicly valued entity, signaling a shift in investor expectations.
The valuation is heavily tied to the company's perceived potential in Artificial Intelligence.
The narrative focuses on the future trajectory of SpaceX now that it is under public investor scrutiny.

stories.title: $130 billion in data center projects blocked by protests so far this year
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Significant investment in data centers ($130 billion) is facing public resistance.
The story highlights the growing political impact of large-scale AI infrastructure projects.
Protests are successfully influencing the development and deployment of AI data centers.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning.
Conventional retrieval methods (lexical/semantic similarity) fail when reasoning requires different strategies for similar problems.
It introduces Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT) as a new post-training framework to improve reasoning.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Mana is a new sim-to-real framework designed to address the complex challenge of dexterous manipulation.
The research specifically tackles the difficulty of coordinating internal degrees of freedom when using articulated tools, a gap in prior work.
It aims to enable functional grasping and manipulation policies for physically complex, articulated tools.

stories.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
stories.author: Min-Hung Chen
bullet_points: The research addresses the fundamental challenge of spatial reasoning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
It focuses on how the 'action interface' used to invoke specialist perception tools limits agentic spatial reasoning capabilities.
The work investigates the impact of interface design on an agent's capacity for open-ended 3D reasoning.

stories.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
stories.author: Stefan Feuerriegel
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the assessment of research reproducibility in social and behavioral sciences.
This approach aims to overcome the resource-intensive and difficult-to-scale nature of traditional reproducibility checks.
The study tested the LLM's performance by comparing its analysis against original data from 76 published studies.

stories.title: Before You Think: System 0, AI-Mediated Cognition and Cognitive Colonization
stories.author: Giuseppe Riva
bullet_points: The paper analyzes three frameworks (Tri-System Theory, Thinkframes, System 0) regarding AI's impact on cognition.
It posits that System 0 offers a unique theoretical perspective not fully captured by the other models.
The core concept introduced is 'cognitive colonization,' describing how AI systems influence human thought processes.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly deepening coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper re-frames 'shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical instrument.
It argues that the underlying automata-theoretic machinery provides structural insights into adversarial networks rather than just restricting agent actions during execution.
Key techniques mentioned include specification compilation, product game construction, and attractor computation.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous robotics, specifically articulated tool manipulation.
Introduces 'Mana' (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework to tackle this complexity.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies for articulated tools.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method tests if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived from composing a provided decomposition of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by leveraging formal structure (Operad theory) rather than just sampling or self-evaluation.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, providing deep coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
stories.author: Bhavika Melwani
bullet_points: Investigates the geometric principles governing knowledge recoverability in continual learning, moving beyond the concept of catastrophic forgetting.
Introduces 'Recovery Subspace Dimensionality' ($k_t$) as a new metric to quantify the complexity of recovering past knowledge.
Employs Split CIFAR-100 and ResNet-18 to analyze representational drift and recoverability across multiple sequential tasks.

stories.title: Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model
stories.author: Anthony Wong
bullet_points: Presents a hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata model for predicting spatially varying wildfire spread.
Develops an optimization framework to design effective aerial suppression strategies.
Addresses operational uncertainty by integrating fire spread prediction with targeted intervention planning.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper re-frames 'Shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It argues that the underlying automata-theoretic machinery provides structural insights into adversarial networks rather than just imposing action constraints.
The methodology involves using specification compilation, product game construction, and attractor computation for analysis.

stories.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
stories.author: Zhiyuan Hu
bullet_points: Introduces EvoArena, a new benchmark suite designed to test LLM agents in dynamic environments.
Addresses a critical gap in current LLM evaluations, which often assume static, non-real-world conditions.
Focuses on the need for agents to continuously adapt their knowledge and behavior to progressive environmental changes.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning.
Proposes a novel framework called Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT).
Argues that simple lexical/semantic similarity fails to capture underlying reasoning patterns in complex problems.

stories.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
stories.author: Pascal Denis
bullet_points: The research introduces 'Influcoder,' a method to create an encoder for Data Attribution (DA) by distilling gradient influence rankings from decoders.
Data Attribution methods are crucial for curating high-quality LLM training datasets by identifying which samples influence model behavior.
The technique addresses the need to trace specific model outputs (e.g., toxic behavior) back to their originating samples in the training data.

stories.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the 'execution-granularity mismatch' in tool-augmented LLM agents, where atomic tool calls clutter the reasoning trace.
Introduces 'HyperTool,' a unified, executable MCP-style tool interface designed to abstract low-level dataflow from the LLM's view.
Aims to improve context efficiency and reduce the cognitive load on the model by moving beyond simple step-wise tool invocation.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method leverages operad theory—a formalism for iterated substitution—to check if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived from a decomposed query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency and semantic entropy) for evaluating LLM reliability during inference.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for Slovak.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly increasing coverage depth for the low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models on embedding tasks.

stories.title: Operads for compositional reasoning in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'operads' as a mathematical framework to formally model question decomposition in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Question decomposition, a common technique to boost LLM reasoning, currently lacks a rigorous mathematical foundation.
The proposed 'questions operad' ($Q$) provides a structure to describe how complex queries are broken down and composed into final answers.

stories.title: repo-slopscore: Detecting AI/LLM contributions in git repositories via commit history analysis
stories.author: slopscan.ava.pet by ava
bullet_points: Introduces 'repo-slopscore,' a tool designed to detect contributions made by AI/LLMs within Git repositories.
The method relies on analyzing the commit history of the codebase to infer AI involvement.
The tool is presented as a self-promoted open-source project available on Codeberg.

stories.title: The Five-Layer Operating System — A Decision Framework for the AI Era
stories.author: lanternproton
bullet_points: The article proposes a 'Five-Layer Operating System' framework.
This framework is designed to help users understand the impact of AI in the modern era.
It frames the current AI development as a 'layer race' rather than just a tool race.

stories.title: The Harness is Half the Architecture. Here's the Half That's Missing.
stories.author: bala_paranj_059d338e44e7e
bullet_points: The article discusses the architecture of AI agents, referencing Vivek Trivedy's 'Anatomy of an Agent Harness'.
A recent NIST mathematical proof established a formal ceiling for the guardrail model used in these agent systems.
The core argument is that a crucial, currently absent layer in the agent architecture is being highlighted and its importance is being analyzed.

stories.title: Claude Code Slash Commands & CLAUDE.md: How I Automate Project Setup, Architecture Enforcement, and Code Reviews
stories.author: vivekjangid
bullet_points: The article details automating software development workflows using Claude Code.
Key features include custom CLAUDE.md files and reusable slash commands for scaffolding.
The setup aims to enforce architectural patterns and automate code reviews within projects.

stories.title: The Hidden Economics of AI: What It Actually Costs to Run LLMs in Production (With Real Data)
stories.author: dalaez
bullet_points: Examines the often-hidden operational costs associated with deploying Large Language Models (LLMs) in real-world production environments.
Promises to provide 'real data' to ground the discussion, suggesting a move beyond theoretical AI hype.
Addresses the economic realities of AI infrastructure, contrasting with the industry's tendency to downplay these expenses.
